title: Groups influencing policy outcomes
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on single-issue groups, social movements, protest movements, interest groups, and government agencies

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6ad3fcf020219c0953fdb9881d9b4991.svg)

**At what stage in the policy process are social movements most successful at impacting policy?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the agenda-setting stage
the policy formulation stage
the policy implementation stage
the policy and program evaluation stage

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best illustrates a social movement directly influencing policymaking?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Several leaders in the LGBTQ Rights movement testify before a congressional committee to report on laws that unfairly apply to transgender people.
Several lobbyists share their policy research with a major swing vote in the House of Representatives to get them to support their policy goals.
The Heritage Foundation releases a report critiquing a proposed policy that would increase federal spending on higher education.
The Department of Transportation establishes several new rules and regulations as they implement a policy recently passed by Congress.

assessment_items.question_content:
| political parties | interest groups
- | - | -
**A** | help raise issues in the agenda-setting stage  | responsible for implementing policy
**B** | aim to get party members elected to the executive and legislature | manage most of the fundraising for political candidates
**C** | indirectly influence public policy through party members | are one of the three participants that make up an iron triangle
**D** | influence policy and program evaluation | influence policy formulation

**Which of the following makes a correct comparison between political parties and interest groups in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
| political parties | protest movements
- | - | -
**A** | are one of the three participants that make up an iron triangle  | aim to get those sympathetic to the movement elected to office
**B** | offer packages of policies to attract voters | bring attention to issues in the agenda-setting stage
**C** | connect individuals to government as a linkage institution | affect the outcome of certain policies as successes or failures
**D** | directly influence the formulation of policy | indirectly influence the implementation of policy

**Which of the following makes a correct comparison between political parties and protest movements in the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following makes a correct comparison between interest groups and social movements in the United States?**

 | interest groups | social movements
- | - | -
**A** | prohibited from donating to political campaigns | draws congressional and media attention to certain issues
**B** | responsible for conducting research to influence policy | responsible for evaluating the success of policy implementation
**C** | acquire voters for members of Congress who share similar policy goals | directly influence policy through protests at the Capitol
**D** | aim to influence the formulation and implementation of policy | aim to influence which problems the government prioritizes


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Comparison A
Comparison B
Comparison C
Comparison D

assessment_items.question_content:
**Political scientists use the term “political realignment” to describe which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A shift in party control of both the presidency and Congress
A major change of a plank in a political party’s platform
The creation or death of political parties
A profound shift in the balance of power between major political parties

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f40ef7c9275e43d40cbaf3c532afebc9.jpg)

Source: Office of Management and Budget

**Based on the above chart, approximately what percent was spent on elective defense or other elective federal programs in the 2016 federal budget?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f40ef7c9275e43d40cbaf3c532afebc9.jpg)

Source: Office of Management and Budget

**At what point in the federal budget process does the Defense Department have the most input?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: When the congressional conference committee submits the passed budget from both houses to the president
When conference committee resolution meetings resolve overall funding differences
When the House Appropriations Committee assigns separate parts of discretionary spending to thirteen subcommittees for review
When executive branch agencies submit budgets for review before the president’s proposal is submitted to Congress

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/722fd61a3bddb4e374129f0598d911258f848a29)

Source: [OpenSecrets.org](https://www.opensecrets.org/industries/totals.php?cycle=2018&ind=Q)

**Based on the above bar graph, which of the following best describes the trend in ideology/single-issue group contributions from 1990 to 2016?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a significant increase of ideology/single-issue contributions
Soft/outside money was non-existent prior to the year 2010
There is little increase in contributions from ideology/single-issue PACs
There is a steady and consistent increase in ideology/single-issue contributions from individuals

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/722fd61a3bddb4e374129f0598d911258f848a29)

Source: [OpenSecrets.org](https://www.opensecrets.org/industries/totals.php?cycle=2018&ind=Q)

Contributions legalized by the Supreme Court’s decision in _Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission_ (2010) are listed in the graph’s "soft/outside money" column, referring to funds not subject to federal campaign finance regulations.

**Which of the following is the best explanation for the change in contributions from 2010 to 2016?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Supreme Court’s 2010 decision appears to have had a positive impact on soft/outside contributions to single issue or partisan recipients, leading to a significant funding increase
Soft/outside money contributions to single issue or partisan recipients decreased in relation to other sources from 2010 to 2016
There does not appear to be any relationship between the change in soft/outside money contributions to single issue or partisan recipients and the Court’s decision in 2010
There is a steady and consistent increase in soft/outside money contributions to single issue or partisan recipients that continued from 2010 to 2016

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements accurately summarizes what the National Rifle Association, American Association of Retired Persons, the National Right to Life Committee, Reproductive Rights Action League, and Mothers Against Drunk Driving (MADD) all have in common?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They all represent citizen groups attempting to influence government policies that they believe will benefit the public at large
They all represent advocacy groups seeking to benefit people unable to represent themselves
They all represent professional associations and businesses concerned with public policies that affect their members
They all represent cause groups representing members that care intensely about a single issue or small group of related issues

assessment_items.question_content:
The presidential election of 1968 was conducted against a backdrop of assassinations, race riots, widespread demonstrations against the Vietnam war, and violent confrontations between police and anti-war protesters at the Democratic National Convention. 

It also included a strong third party effort by former Alabama Governor George Wallace, a pro-segregation Democrat running as independent. The election outcome was a victory for GOP candidate Richard Nixon, with Wallace receiving 46 electoral votes from southern states. 

**Which of the following terms accurately characterizes this election and political era?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Disenfranchisement
Divided government
A party era
Party realignment
